text.title: pytest Fixtures — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when python-pytest-fixtures is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.description: session-scope DB fixture creating data tests then mutate.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Tests bleed into each other; order matters. Fix: Use function-scope with savepoints/rollbacks.

antipattern.description: Fixture opens a connection and returns it (no yield).
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Connection leaks; eventually exhaust the pool. Fix: Use yield; close in the after-yield block.

antipattern.description: @pytest.fixture(autouse=True, scope='function') that builds a large dataset.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Every test pays the cost even if unused. Fix: Drop autouse; inject explicitly where needed.

antipattern.description: Fixture appends to a module-level list with no reset.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Tests pass alone, fail together. Fix: Reset state in the teardown half of the fixture.

antipattern.description: Fixture A depends on B, B depends on A.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: pytest raises 'Recursive dependency in fixtures'. Fix: Break the cycle: extract shared piece into C.
